mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to explain more than 15% of SPX return variation in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with low interest rates indicates robust manufacturing activity and favorable financing conditions, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive returns in 40%+ of cases during the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% prompts central bank rate hikes that increase corporate borrowing costs and reduce earnings visibility, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth signals robust employment conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates elevated consumer and business demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST explains less than 10% of SPX return variation in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production during high inflationary environments signals resilient supply chains and pricing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive returns in 35%+ of cases during the 60-day horizon.
